ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for yakitori donburi with daikon and ponzu. FOODON_00002572 (chicken dish) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Chicken meat dishes) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for yakitori donburi with daikon and ponzu sauce.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific Japanese rice bowl dish.

allergens: wheat (ponzu sauce typically contains soy sauce with wheat)
fish (ponzu contains citrus juice and often dashi from bonito)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 380 g, comprising a bowl of steamed Japanese short-grain rice topped with grilled chicken yakitori skewers, diced and grated daikon radish, and ponzu sauce drizzled over the top.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, broilers or fryers, thigh, meat only, cooked, roasted (USDA SR Legacy 05178) combined with Cooked white rice, short-grain (USDA SR Legacy 20450) and Daikon radish, raw (USDA SR Legacy 11429); ponzu sauce component estimated from USDA Branded Foods and standard Japanese condiment composition
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component assembly: approximately 35% cooked chicken thigh (USDA SR Legacy 05178), 50% cooked short-grain white rice (USDA SR Legacy 20450), 10% daikon radish raw (USDA SR Legacy 11429), and 5% ponzu sauce (estimated from USDA Branded Foods ponzu sauce entries). All component values drawn from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Weighted per-100g values calculated from these proportions.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white rice, Japanese short-grain, cooked (GI ~54–56); chicken and daikon components contribute negligible GI
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite dish. GI is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate source: Japanese short-grain white rice, which has a GI of approximately 54–56 per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021). Chicken, daikon, and ponzu sauce contribute negligible glycemic load. Composite GI estimated at 54 (low). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (54 × 79.0) / 100 ≈ 42.7, rounded to 42.6. Carbohydrate per serving = 20.8 g/100g × 380 g = 79.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from ponzu sauce and soy-based marinade
hypertension.guidance: Each serving provides approximately 1178 mg sodium (310 mg/100g × 380 g), which represents roughly 51% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sodium ponzu or limit portion size to manage daily sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load from white rice portion
type2_diabetes.guidance: The glycemic load per serving is approximately 42.6, which is high; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider reducing the rice portion, substituting with brown rice or cauliflower rice, and pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Lean grilled chicken with low saturated fat and beneficial daikon phytonutrients
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Grilled chicken thigh provides a relatively lean protein source with approximately 0.8 g saturated fat per 100 g, and daikon radish contributes dietary fibre and vitamin C; the primary cardiovascular concern is the sodium load from ponzu and soy-based sauces, which should be moderated in individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Yakitori Bowl with Diced and Grated Daikon and Ponzu Sauce is a Japanese donburi (rice bowl) dish consisting of grilled chicken yakitori skewers served over steamed Japanese short-grain white rice, topped with a combination of finely diced and freshly grated daikon radish, and finished with a drizzle of ponzu sauce. Yakitori refers to bite-sized pieces of chicken — typically thigh meat — skewered and grilled over charcoal or a gas grill, often basted with a tare (seasoning sauce) of soy sauce, mirin, and sake. Ponzu is a Japanese citrus-based sauce combining soy sauce with yuzu or other citrus juice, giving the dish a bright, tangy, and umami-rich flavour profile. The grated daikon (daikon oroshi) adds a cool, mildly peppery, and refreshing contrast to the savoury grilled chicken, while the diced daikon provides textural variation. This dish is commonly served in Japanese casual dining restaurants and fast-casual chains in Singapore. Nutritionally, the bowl provides a moderate energy density of approximately 148 kcal per 100 g, with a balanced macronutrient profile of roughly 9.2 g protein, 3.1 g fat, and 20.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g. The primary clinical consideration is the sodium content contributed by ponzu and soy-based marinades, which may be a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load is high per serving due to the white rice base, making portion control important for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is dairy-free and provides useful amounts of niacin, vitamin B6, and potassium from the chicken component.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component assembly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked chicken thigh, cooked short-grain white rice, raw daikon radish, and ponzu sauce. GI is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate source (Japanese short-grain white rice) using the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible but not derived from a direct measurement of this dish.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated: approximately 35% cooked chicken thigh (USDA SR Legacy 05178), 50% cooked short-grain white rice (USDA SR Legacy 20450), 10% raw daikon radish (USDA SR Legacy 11429), and 5% ponzu sauce (USDA Branded Foods estimate). 2. trans_fat_g is set to 0.0 as grilled chicken and rice-based dishes contain negligible industrially produced trans fats; confirmed negligible per USDA SR Legacy component data. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component sources used for this estimation. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component sources used for this estimation. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component sources used for this estimation. 6. GI estimated at 54 from Japanese short-grain white rice per University of Sydney GI Database; gi_category is low (GI 54 falls in the low-to-medium boundary; classified as low per the <55 threshold). 7. Glycemic load per serving = (54 × 79.0 g carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 42.7, reported as 42.6. 8. Sodium estimate of 310 mg/100g reflects the ponzu and soy-based marinade contribution; actual sodium may vary by restaurant preparation. 9. The food_name has been normalised from the field survey menu title '476. Diced & Grated Daikon Chicken Yakitori Bowl with Ponzu Sauce' to a canonical English form. 10. Halal status is not confirmed; ponzu sauce may contain mirin (alcohol) and dashi from bonito, making halal certification uncertain. 11. FoodOn URI FOODON_00002572 and FoodEx2 code A0CKP are used as closest parent matches; no exact ontology code exists for this specific dish.
